meta_title: For Founders: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code for SaaS founders.
content.answer_block: Overcoming a lack of portfolio requires demonstrating velocity, not just finished apps. By leveraging Claude Code to automate boilerplate and Antigravity to structure complex logic, you can ship high-fidelity MVP features in hours. This workflow proves technical competence, accelerates development cycles, and turns experimental ideas into tangible, revenue-ready assets.
content.intro:
The SaaS graveyard is littered with brilliant ideas that never saw the light of day because the founder was paralyzed by the 'portfolio gap.' You have the vision, but the market demands proof—they want to see code that works, architecture that scales, and features that solve real problems. Without a track record, you are just another dreamer in a sea of noise.

But the game has changed. With Claude Code acting as your force-multiplier and Antigravity streamlining your architectural foundation, you no longer need a decade of experience to build like a senior engineer. This isn't about writing more code; it's about deploying logic with surgical precision. Let’s turn your 'no portfolio' problem into a competitive advantage by shipping your first production-grade feature today.

steps.title: Define the High-Value MVP Feature
steps.description: Don't build a generic app. Identify a specific, painful problem in your niche and define a single feature that solves it. This creates a focused, impressive portfolio piece.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Define a high-impact MVP feature for a SaaS targeting [Niche]. Outline the core data schema, API endpoints, and the primary user flow required to demonstrate technical competence.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture with Claude Code
steps.description: Initialize your project structure using Claude Code to ensure industry-standard directory layouts and dependency management from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready project structure for a Next.js and TypeScript SaaS application. Include folder conventions for services, hooks, and database models.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "initialize a professional project structure with standard boilerplate"

steps.title: Automate Business Logic with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate complex business logic modules, ensuring your code is modular, testable, and clean—the hallmark of an expert engineer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust backend service module for handling user authentication and data validation using Zod schemas. Ensure clean error handling and type safety.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "implement the auth service based on the generated architecture"

steps.title: Implement Rapid UI Components
steps.description: Build the frontend layer quickly by prompting for accessible, responsive components that mirror modern SaaS design patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a reusable dashboard component layout using Tailwind CSS. Include placeholders for data visualization and action buttons.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "build the dashboard component and integrate with the service layer"

steps.title: Refine and Document for Impact
steps.description: Your portfolio piece is only as good as its documentation. Use Claude Code to generate a professional README that explains your technical decisions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional README.md for this project that highlights the technical challenges solved, the tech stack used, and the business outcome of the feature.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "generate a comprehensive project README and document the API endpoints"

pro_tips: Treat your commit history as your resume; write atomic, descriptive commit messages to show future investors your engineering discipline.
Always run Claude Code with a strict 'lint-first' policy to ensure your generated code passes CI checks immediately.
Use Antigravity to generate unit tests for your core business logic; nothing proves seniority faster than a high-coverage test suite.
Keep your Claude Code context windows clean by deleting unused files before starting a new feature branch.
Focus on one 'hero' feature that solves a hard engineering problem, like real-time data syncing or complex state management, rather than a generic CRUD app.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel or Railway, record a 60-second Loom demo of the feature in action, and share it on LinkedIn or X to begin building your public reputation.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect moment to build your portfolio. Every line of code you ship today is a step toward your first MRR. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
